titleStmt.title: The Chargé in China ( Atcheson ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Chargé in China ( Atcheson ) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
334. ReDeptel 1522, November 23, 5 p.m.;
Foreign Relations, 1944, vol. vi, p. 1087. Embstel 1978, December 8, 11 a.m.,
Ibid., p. 1093. and Embdes 15, December 18.
Ibid., p. 1094.
Foreign Affairs Political Vice MinisterK. C. Wu. handed to me on February 23 brief oral statement on commercial policy of Chinese Government summarized as follows:

Chinese Government voluntarily supports free trade policy for which in recent years U. S. Government has energetically sought general acceptance and implementation, both at home and abroad, and earnestly seeks unremitting collaboration. When the Chinese Government establishes a postwar commercial policy it will be based on principles of reciprocity and equality in accordance with concept of international economic collaboration enunciated in Atlantic CharterJoint declaration by President Roosevelt and British Prime Minister Churchill, August 14, 1941; for text, see Foreign Relations, 1941, vol. i, p. 367. as well as in spirit of article 7 of Mutual Aid Agreement between Chinese and U. S. Governments.Signed at Washington, June 2, 1942, Executive Agreement Series No. 251, or 56 Stat. (pt. 2) 1494.
